info.title: Microsoft (R) WCF Service Model Proxy Generation Tool for .Net Core platform
info.description:
[Microsoft.Tools.ServiceModel.Svcutil, Version 8.0.0]
Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.  All rights reserved.
This tool collects information about how it is used in order to improve the tool. This functionality can be disabled by setting the environment variable "DOTNET_SVCUTIL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT" to 1.
USE:
- Generate web service proxy code from running services or static metadata documents.
-= USAGE =-
<metadata document path> - The path to a metadata document (wsdl or xsd).
Standard command-line wildcards can be used in the
file path.
<url>                    - The URL to a service endpoint that provides metadata                            or to a metadata document hosted online.
<epr>                    - The path to an XML file that contains a
WS-Addressing EndpointReference for a service
endpoint that supports WS-Metadata Exchange.

options.name: --outputDir
options.description:
- Directory to create files in.
Default: A directory called
ServiceReference inside the current                                             directory. (Short Form: -d)

options.name: --noLogo
options.description:
- Suppress the copyright and banner
message. (Short Form: -nl)

options.name: --verbosity
options.description:
- Determines the amount of information                                            displayed by the tool. Valid values                                             are 'Silent, Minimal, Normal,
Verbose, Debug'. (Short Form: -v)

options.name: --help
options.description:
- Display command syntax and options
for the tool. (Short Form: -h)

options.name: --projectFile
options.description:
- The project file to add the client
to (if any). (Short Form: -pf).

options.name: --outputFile
options.description:
- The filename for the generated code.                                            Default: derived from the WSDL
definition name, WSDL service name
or targetNamespace of one of the
schemas. (Short Form: -o)

options.name: --namespace
options.description:
- A mapping from a WSDL or XML Schema                                             targetNamespace to a CLR namespace.                                             Using the '*' for the
targetNamespace maps all
targetNamespaces without an explicit                                            mapping to that CLR namespace.
Default: derived from the target
namespace of the schema document for                                            Data Contracts. The default
namespace is used for all other
generated types. (Short Form: -n)

options.name: --messageContract
options.description:
- Generate Message Contract types.
(Short Form: -mc)

options.name: --enableDataBinding
options.description:
- Implement the System.ComponentModel.
INotifyPropertyChanged interface on                                             all Data Contract types to enable
data binding. (Short Form: -edb)

options.name: --internal
options.description:
- Generate classes that are marked as                                             internal. Default: generate public
classes. (Short Form: -i)

options.name: --reference
options.description:
- Package or project references to
reuse types from. When generating
clients, use this option to specify                                             references in the user's project
that might contain types
representing the metadata being
imported. The reference can be
specified as a nuget package name
and version or the path to a .Net
Core project. (Short Form: -r)

options.name: --noTypeReuse
options.description:
- Disable reusing types from project
refereces. References provided with                                             the --reference option will only be                                             considered for resolving collection                                             types specified with the

options.name: --collectionType
options.description: Form: -ntr)

options.name: --collectionType
options.description:
- A fully-qualified or
assembly-qualified name of the type                                             to use as a collection data type
when code is generated from schemas.                                            (Short Form: -ct)

options.name: --excludeType
options.description:
- A fully-qualified or
assembly-qualified type name to
exclude from referenced contract
types. (Short Form: -et)

options.name: --noStdLib
options.description:
- Do not reference standard libraries.                                            By default System.Runtime (mscorlib)                                            and System.ServiceModel (WCF)
libraries are referenced. (Short
Form: -nsl)

options.name: --serializer
options.description:
- Automatically select the serializer.                                            This tries to use the Data Contract                                             serializer and uses the
XmlSerializer if that fails. (Short                                             Form: -ser)

options.name: --serializer
options.description:
- Generate data types that use the
Data Contract Serializer for
serialization and deserialization

options.name: --serializer
options.description:
- Generate data types that use the
XmlSerializer for serialization and                                             deserialization

options.name: --sync
options.description:
- Generate synchronous methods for
operations in addition to async.
(Short Form: -syn)

options.name: --syncOnly
options.description:
- Generate only synchronous methods
for operations. Default: generate
task-based asynchronous method
signatures. (Short Form: -so)

options.name: --wrapped
options.description:
- Generated code will not unwrap
"parameters" member of
document-wrapped-literal messages.
(Short Form: -wr)

options.name: --update
options.description:
- Updates an existing web service
reference. If the project contains
more than one web service reference,                                            the name of the web service
reference to be updated is required.                                            (Short Form: -u)

options.name: --runtimeIdentifier
options.description:
building reference projects (if
any). (Short Form: -ri)

options.name: --targetFramework
options.description:
- The target framework used for
building reference projects. (Short                                             Form: -tf).

options.name: --acceptCertificate
options.description:
- Accept remote certificate as trusted                                            when downloading service metadata.
(Short Form: -ac)

options.name: --serviceContract
options.description:
- Generate code for Service Contracts.                                            Client class will not be generated.                                             (Short Form: -sc)
-= EXAMPLES =-
dotnet-svcutil http://example.com/service.svc?wsdl
- Generate client code from a running service or online metadata documents.
dotnet-svcutil http://example.com/service.svc?wsdl -edb -n "*,MyApplication.ServiceReference1" -ct MyLibrary.MyCollection`1 -r "{{MyLibrary,1.0.0}}"
- Generate client code from a running service using settings that emulate
the default "WCF Connected Service" behavior in Visual Studio
dotnet-svcutil -u ServiceReference
- Update existing web service reference named ServiceReference. The command     need to be executed under directory which contains the project file.
